From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Ski (producer)](/w/index.php?title=Ski_(producer)&redirect=no "Ski (producer)"))

American record producer from North Carolina

Not to be confused with [E-A-Ski](/wiki/E-A-Ski "E-A-Ski").

Musical artist

**David Anthony Willis**, known professionally as **Ski Beatz**, is an American [record producer](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer") from [Greensboro, North Carolina](/wiki/Greensboro,_North_Carolina "Greensboro, North Carolina"), mainly working in hip hop.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ski_Beatz&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Discovered by [DJ Clark Kent](/wiki/DJ_Clark_Kent "DJ Clark Kent"),[[2]](#cite_note-clarkkent-2) Ski was originally known as "MC Will-Ski" in the late 1980s. He began his recording career in one of [North Carolina](/wiki/North_Carolina "North Carolina")'s first hip hop groups *The Bizzie Boyz*, alongside Mixmasta "D" (Dana Owens, also from NC), [Fanatic](/wiki/Andreao_Heard "Andreao Heard"), and dancers Move & Groove. In the early 1990s, Ski moved to [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") and was a member of the group [Original Flavor](/wiki/Original_Flavor "Original Flavor"), the first group managed by future recording industry executive [Damon Dash](/wiki/Damon_Dash "Damon Dash").[[3]](#cite_note-ProdCorner-3) In the mid-90s, Ski was working on duo [Camp Lo](/wiki/Camp_Lo "Camp Lo")'s debut album *[Uptown Saturday Night](/wiki/Uptown_Saturday_Night_(album) "Uptown Saturday Night (album)")* when Dash called him in to work on the debut album of his own artist, [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z"). Jay-Z's album, *[Reasonable Doubt](/wiki/Reasonable_Doubt_(album) "Reasonable Doubt (album)")*, was released in 1996, marking Ski's first major production placement. Ski produced four tracks off the album, including the singles "[Dead Presidents](/wiki/Dead_Presidents_(song) "Dead Presidents (song)")" and "[Feelin' It](/wiki/Feelin%27_It_(Jay-Z_song) "Feelin' It (Jay-Z song)")".

After the critical success of *Reasonable Doubt*, Ski formed Roc-A-Blok Productions in affiliation with Jay and Dame's label, [Roc-A-Fella Records](/wiki/Roc-A-Fella_Records "Roc-A-Fella Records"), working with Camp Lo and [Sporty Thievz](/wiki/Sporty_Thievz "Sporty Thievz"). The producer crafted most of Camp Lo's debut, which dropped in 1997, and produced the [Fat Joe](/wiki/Fat_Joe "Fat Joe") single "John Blaze"; Ski continued to work with Jay-Z, on his second album *[In My Lifetime, Vol. 1](/wiki/In_My_Lifetime,_Vol._1 "In My Lifetime, Vol. 1")* as well as non-solo albums and compilation projects, until 1998, when Jay released his third project *[Vol. 2... Hard Knock Life](/wiki/Vol._2..._Hard_Knock_Life "Vol. 2... Hard Knock Life")* without beats from any of his former producers except for [DJ Premier](/wiki/DJ_Premier "DJ Premier").

Though Roc-A-Blok Productions had since folded, Ski remained somewhat active in music. Relocating to his home state of [North Carolina](/wiki/North_Carolina "North Carolina") for a break from [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City")'s fast-paced lifestyle, he continued producing for artists such as [Nature](/wiki/Nature_(rapper) "Nature (rapper)"), members of the [New Jersey](/wiki/New_Jersey "New Jersey") crew The Outsidaz, [Lil' Kim](/wiki/Lil%27_Kim "Lil' Kim"), [Foxy Brown](/wiki/Foxy_Brown_(rapper) "Foxy Brown (rapper)"), [Ras Kass](/wiki/Ras_Kass "Ras Kass") and [Proof](/wiki/Proof_(rapper) "Proof (rapper)"). The producer has also continued to produce for Camp Lo, providing most of the production for their second album *Let's Do it Again*, as well as their third album, *Black Hollywood*.

In 2010, Ski linked back up with Dame Dash, joining the entrepreneur's [DD172](/wiki/DD172 "DD172") collective, subsequently nicknamed the 24-Hour Karate School. Since the foundation of the studio enclave, Ski has worked with [Mos Def](/wiki/Mos_Def "Mos Def"), [Jay Electronica](/wiki/Jay_Electronica "Jay Electronica"), [Jean Grae](/wiki/Jean_Grae "Jean Grae") and others. On April 30, 2010, he announced that he would produce upcoming albums for Jean Grae, [Joell Ortiz](/wiki/Joell_Ortiz "Joell Ortiz"), and [Ras Kass](/wiki/Ras_Kass "Ras Kass"), as well as all but three songs on [Curren$y](/wiki/Curren$y "Curren$y")'s third album [Pilot Talk](/wiki/Pilot_Talk "Pilot Talk"). All of these artists were also later featured on the producer's solo debut, titled *[24 Hour Karate School](/wiki/24_Hour_Karate_School "24 Hour Karate School")* in honor of the collective.[[3]](#cite_note-ProdCorner-3)

On July 14, 2012, Ski announced he had secured his own label deal, named "The Fresh Air Fund Music Group".

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ski_Beatz&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Discography")]

Main article: [Ski Beatz production discography](/wiki/Ski_Beatz_production_discography "Ski Beatz production discography")

**Studio albums**

* *[24 Hour Karate School](/wiki/24_Hour_Karate_School "24 Hour Karate School")* (2010)
* *24 Hour Karate School 2* (2011)
* *24 Hour Karate School Presents: Twilight* (2012)

**Collaboration albums**

* *[Love & Rockets Vol. 1: The Transformation](/wiki/Love_%26_Rockets_Vol._1:_The_Transformation "Love & Rockets Vol. 1: The Transformation")* (with [Murs](/wiki/Murs_(rapper) "Murs (rapper)")) (2011)
* *Embedded* (with [Locksmith](/wiki/Locksmith_(rapper) "Locksmith (rapper)")) (2011)[[4]](#cite_note-4)
